falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: h_A2_001: replacing workflow dispatch with scheduler dispatch preserves correctness using existing leases/idempotency
falsificationQueries: convex scheduler atomicity differences mutation vs action
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Convex guarantees atomic scheduling only when `runAfter` is called from a mutation. Scheduling from an action is explicitly non-atomic, and scheduled actions execute at-most-once with no automatic retry. If the migration introduces or preserves action-based dispatch boundaries, lease guards alone do not guarantee no-drop/no-double-apply correctness.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: h_A2_002: scheduler-only needs explicit throttling/chunking to stay within platform limits under bursts
falsificationQueries: whether Convex limits are soft enough to avoid explicit throttling
scheduled function and concurrency hard limits
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Primary docs show hard ceilings (e.g., max 1000 functions scheduled per mutation, capped outstanding scheduled count, and global scheduled jobs concurrency). This supports rather than disproves the need for throttling. Null challenge attempt did not overturn the hypothesis.
